symptoms.symptom_name: Fiery-red, sharply demarcated, raised, tender advancing facial plaque with peau d'orange

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sharply demarcated raised fiery plaque with abrupt fever is characteristic of erysipelas.
Lupus malar rash is flat, non-tender and afebrile.
These would indicate orbital involvement requiring emergency care; require in-person exam to fully exclude.
Absence lowers but does not exclude necrotising fasciitis — remains a safety-net.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person assessment ± bloods (FBC, CRP, lactate, cultures)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess sepsis and guide IV antibiotics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT orbits/sinuses if orbital involvement suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To evaluate orbital cellulitis/cavernous sinus thrombosis.
